name: Tajuña Greenway
name_original: Vía Verde del Tajuña – de Arganda del Rey a Morata de Tajuña
description: Cycle the 16.6-mile (26.7 km) Via Verde del Tajuna, a moderate touring route from Arganda del Rey to Morata de Tajuna.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Arganda Del Rey offers a mix of natural landscapes and dedicated cycling infrastructure. The region is situated within the Southeast Regional Park, featuring agricultural areas, Mediterranean forests, and riverine wetlands formed by the Jarama and Tajuña rivers. Terrain varies from flat greenways to routes with moderate elevation changes, providing diverse options for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Arganda Del Rey The most popular touring cycling route is Tajuña Greenway, a 16.6 miles (26.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a converted railway line through the Tajuña Valley. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Morata de Tajuña Town Square – Tajuña Greenway Section loop from Arganda del Rey, a moderate 28.4 miles (45.6 km) path. This route includes sections of the Tajuña Greenway, offering varied scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Tajuña Greenway Section – Tajuña Greenway loop from Arganda del Rey, a 59.0 miles (94.9 km) trail leading through the Tajuña Valley, often completed in about 5 hours 38 minutes. Touring cycling around Arganda Del Rey is defined by riverine wetlands, Mediterranean forests, and converted railway lines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging excursions. The routes in Arganda Del Rey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Arganda Del Rey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Arganda Del Rey?
A: Arganda Del Rey offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 70 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 4 easy, 37 moderate, and 29 difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Arganda Del Rey?
A: The terrain around Arganda Del Rey is quite varied, ranging from relatively flat greenways to routes with moderate elevation changes. You'll cycle through agricultural areas, Mediterranean forests, and riverine wetlands formed by the Jarama and Tajuña rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961442446/tajuna-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, for example, follows a converted railway line, offering a mostly paved and accessible path through the scenic Tajuña Valley.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Arganda Del Rey?
A: Yes, Arganda Del Rey has routes suitable for beginners and families. The Vía Verde del Tajuña is an excellent option, being a converted railway line that is mostly paved and offers a moderate touring experience. While the guide lists 4 easy routes, many of the moderate trails, especially sections of the greenway, can be enjoyed by those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: Are there more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a greater challenge, there are 29 difficult touring cycling routes in the area. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20174511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway Section – Tajuña Greenway loop from Arganda del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 94.9 km (59.0 miles) trail that typically takes over 5 hours to complete and features significant elevation changes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes in Arganda Del Rey pass through or near significant natural features and landmarks. You can explore the diverse ecosystems of the Southeast Regional Park, including the Jarama River and its tributaries. Notable natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2620629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5123913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarama Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; with their specialized flora, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4232941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivas Cliffs&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also offer panoramic views from points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2864570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the confluence of the Jarama and Manzanares rivers from the Cortados de Rivas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Arganda Del Rey?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes available. These loops allow you to start and end your ride in the same location, offering convenience. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morata de Tajuña Town Square – Tajuña Greenway Section loop from Arganda del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 45.6 km (28.4 miles) and incorporates sections of the popular Tajuña Greenway.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Arganda Del Rey?
A: The touring cycling routes in Arganda Del Rey are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the Tajuña Valley, and the well-maintained sections of the Vía Verde del Tajuña. The mix of natural landscapes and dedicated cycling infrastructure makes it a popular choice.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes in Arganda Del Rey using public transport?
A: Arganda del Rey is located just 25 kilometers southeast of Madrid, making it accessible. While specific public transport connections to trailheads might vary, the town itself is connected to Madrid via metro (Line 9) and bus services. This allows for convenient access to the starting points of many routes, especially those originating directly from Arganda del Rey.

Q: Are there places to park my car near the touring cycling routes?
A: Given Arganda del Rey's proximity to Madrid and its popularity for outdoor activities, parking is generally available within the town and at various access points to the Vía Verde del Tajuña and other regional parks. It's advisable to check specific starting points for designated parking areas, especially for longer routes that might begin outside the town center.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Arganda Del Rey?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling in Arganda Del Rey. During these seasons, temperatures are mild and comfortable, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if cycling during these months. Winters are generally mild but can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or cultural attractions?
A: While the focus is on natural landscapes, some routes may offer glimpses of historical elements. Arganda del Rey has an Optical Telegraph Tower, a restored 19th-century structure, and the Cerro del Melero offers panoramic views and holds historical significance from the Battle of Jarama. Additionally, the region is known for its wineries under the 'Vinos de Madrid' Designation of Origin, with some offering tours and tastings that could be combined with a cycling trip.

Q: Is it possible to combine cycling with other activities like exploring lagoons or viewpoints?
A: Yes, the region is perfect for combining cycling with other outdoor activities. Many routes pass by or offer easy detours to natural highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2572064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3915788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miralrío Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, which are excellent for birdwatching or tranquil walks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31486638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo Lagoon – La Poveda Railway Bridge loop from Arganda del Rey&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that takes you past these natural wetlands.
tips: It is an endearing town that is worth visiting!
Old train stop where to load remains of lime and rocks from nearby quarries

Arganda del Rey is, in addition to being a beautiful town whose center is worth visiting, the gateway to the Vía Verde del Tajuña, a beautiful bike path that allows us to visit many towns in the south.

faqs.question: How can I get to the start of the trail in Arganda del Rey?
faqs.answer: The most convenient way to reach the starting point in Arganda del Rey is by using Metro Line 9, which extends directly to Arganda del Rey. If you're driving, you can find parking in the town itself near the former railway station, which is now the Arganda del Rey Station.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly paved surfaces, including a distinctive reddish asphalt in the Madrid section, ensuring a smooth ride. As a 'Vía Verde' (Greenway), it follows an old railway line, meaning it generally maintains mild gradients, typically around 3% maximum. There is a gentle but prolonged 7-kilometer ascent from Arganda, followed by a shorter, steeper descent into Morata de Tajuña.

faqs.question: Is the Via Verde del Tajuna suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, despite being classified as 'moderate' due to some ascents, the trail is largely suitable for all skill levels, including beginners. Its railway origins mean the gradients are mild, making for a peaceful pedal. The mostly paved surfaces also contribute to its accessibility.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter varied landscapes including fertile lowlands, scrubland, and prominent olive groves. Scenic views of the Tajuña valley are notable, especially near a precipice before Morata de Tajuña. Highlights include the Morata de Tajuña Town Square, Cornicabra Station and Quarries, and the Arganda del Rey Station. You'll also pass old water mills, riverside forests, and vestiges of the region's mining and industrial past, including a cement factory and interesting plaster walls with old caves.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle the Via Verde del Tajuna. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures for enjoying the scenery and cycling comfortably.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally permitted on the Via Verde del Tajuna. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and the comfort of other trail users, especially given the shared nature of greenways.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to use the trail or access Parque Regional del Sureste?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Via Verde del Tajuna. As it is part of the Parque Regional del Sureste, access to the greenway itself is free and open to the public. However, always respect local regulations and environmental guidelines within the park.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, a significant portion of the trail overlaps with the Ruta Verde 3.3 Vía Verde del Tajuña, the Camino Santiaguista de Uclés, and the Camino de Uclés. You'll also find smaller sections that coincide with other parts of the Vía Verde del Tajuña and local routes like the Carril Bici - Arganda - La Poveda.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a greenway, you'll find services in the towns at either end. Arganda del Rey and Morata de Tajuña both offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel or grab a snack. It's a good idea to carry water and some provisions, especially for the sections between towns.

faqs.question: What makes this Vía Verde unique compared to others in the Madrid region?
faqs.answer: This Vía Verde holds the distinction of being the longest in the Madrid region and was the first 'Vía Verde' established in the Community of Madrid in 1999. It's notable for its accessibility via Metro Line 9 to Arganda del Rey, and its rich historical context, being part of the old 'Tren de Arganda' railway line. You can observe vestiges of the region's mining and industrial past, as well as areas related to the Spanish Civil War.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 26.7 kilometers long. For a touring bicycle, it typically takes around 2 hours and 27 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or visit points of interest.
